STATEMENT RE OSC

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT GLENBOROUGH NEW MONTGOMERY, LLC'S MOTION FOR AN ORDER TO RECLASSIFY CASE AS LIMITED CIVIL ACTION. NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - DENIED. DEFENDANT HAS FAILED TO DEMONSTRATE THAT PLAINTIFF'S RECOVERY OVER $25,000 IS "VIRTUALLY UNATTAINABLE." (SEE WALKER V. SUP. CT. (1991) 63 CAL.3D 257, 269. PLAINTIFF HAS APPROXIMATELY $12,000 IN MEDICAL BILLS, IS STILL CURRENTLY SEEKING MEDICAL TREATMENT FOR AN UPDATE ON HER MEDICAL CONDITION, AND STILL HAS ATTENDANT PAIN, STRESS, AND RELATED EMOTIONAL DISTRESS DAMAGES RELATED TO THE INCIDENT. (SEE DECLARATION OF ROSA IN SUPPORT OF OPPOSITION TO DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO RECLASSIFY CASE 3-7.) ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: JACQUELINE ALAMEDA; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

Off Calendar Jul-11-2013 Continued To Mar-17-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion For An Order To Reclassify Case As Limited Civil Action
Off Calendar Jul-11-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT GLENBOROUGH NEW MONTGOMERY, LLC'S Motion For An Order To Reclassify Case As Limited Civil Action is denied. Defendant has failed to demonstrate that Plaintiff's recovery over $25,000 is "virtually unattainable." (See Walker v. Sup. Ct. (1991) 63 Cal.3d 257, 269. Plaintiff has approximately $12,000 in medical bills, is still currently seeking medical treatment for an update on her medical condition, and still has attendant pain, stress, and related emotional distress damages related to the incident. (See Declaration of Rosa in Support of Opposition to Defendant's Motion to Reclassify Case 3-7.) Order signed. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda; Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR # 6185.

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Apr-03-2013 Continued To Aug-26-13 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-16-2012 Set For Trial On May-20-13 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206
